key_insights.insight: Procrastination is often driven by psychological discomfort rather than perfectionism, stemming from self-doubt about one's ability to fully execute a task.
key_insights.quote: Procrastination is the result of us trying to avoid a task or thing that's that we have psychological discomfort associated with.

key_insights.insight: Success can be a curse if it leads to unrealistic personal expectations of oneself and the world.
key_insights.quote: Success can be a curse because of the way it messes with our own personal expectations of ourself and of the world.

key_insights.insight: Finding a balance between the real world and the social media world is essential for mental well-being.
key_insights.quote: It's more kind of like finding a balance that's right for you that is going to help you feel better about yourself mentally.

key_insights.insight: Having a support system, like friends or family, can significantly help in navigating the challenges and anxieties that come with a career in social media.
key_insights.quote: It definitely definitely helped having someone so close to me like my sister being able to sort of help with that.

key_insights.insight: Being able to de-catastrophize and using time to separate oneself from anxiety-inducing situations can help manage overwhelming feelings.
key_insights.quote: Using time to separate so like if i'm if i'm feeling anxious i've got a sort of the way you think about time helps.

key_insights.insight: It is important to acknowledge feelings of self-doubt and imposter syndrome rather than allowing them to control your decisions.
key_insights.quote: That imposter syndrome I already had got amplified and I was like, maybe I shouldn't do it.

key_insights.insight: Instant success can warp expectations and make it difficult to appreciate the slower, more organic growth process of new ventures.
key_insights.quote: It's looking for instant success and stuff which is weird and maybe that's because of like before on youtube and stuff there was a time where you know we were...everything we sort of would go into almost became an instant success.

actionable_advice.advice: De-catastrophize anxiety-inducing situations by breaking them down into smaller chunks of time.
actionable_advice.how_to: If facing a long meeting or task, mentally divide it into 30-minute or 1-hour segments to make it feel more manageable. During moments of anxiety, remind yourself the difficult period will be over soon.

actionable_advice.advice: If you are feeling overwhelmed with multiple options, do not automatically focus on the most 'successful' option, but return to things you are genuinely passionate about and find joy in.
actionable_advice.how_to: If you are spinning multiple plates, set aside time each day to engage in activities you enjoy, whether it's sculpting, painting, or spending time in nature. Prioritize these activities, even if only for short periods, to maintain a sense of fulfillment and joy.

actionable_advice.advice: To manage self-doubt, consciously challenge worst-case scenario thinking.
actionable_advice.how_to: When self-doubt arises, actively identify and question the assumptions underlying negative thoughts. Replace those thoughts with more balanced and realistic appraisals of your abilities and the potential outcomes of your efforts.

memorable_stories: Joe's experience roof thatching and deciding to pursue YouTube as a hobby shows the organic way career paths can evolve.

The story of being followed by paparazzi with his then new girlfriend (now partner) Diane in his loaned Aston Martin and having to put on the James Bond theme song shows the loss of privacy with fame.
Joe's story about his manager sending him a care package with a Harry Potter book when he was experiencing burnout illustrates the importance of support and appreciation.
contrarian_claims: Sometimes, real success is being happy. You don't need a gold medal or a gazillion followers.
The reasons people procrastinate are often caused by self-doubt rather than perfectionism.
episode_title: The Diary Of A CEO with Steven Bartlett - Growing A 10+ Million Youtube Following At The Age of 22: Joe Sugg
